summary: Mexico is where my family originally lived before immigrating to the United States. My mom first moved there, and after some years, my brothers and I joined her. I have deep ties to Mexico through my culture, especially through folkloric dance, which I started practicing since I was twelve. My dad introduced me to Mexican folklore, and I cherish the traditions and celebrations we had there.

Minnesota became my family's new home after they immigrated from Mexico. My mom and brothers moved there first, and I joined them five years later. I brought my folkloric dance costumes with me because I wanted to keep practicing, even though I thought it would be hard to find Mexican folklore costumes in Minnesota. Now, I am connected to the Mexican community here and hope to join a local ballet folklore group.

Mexican Independence Day was one of the traditional celebrations where I performed folkloric dances back in Mexico. These events were important for me to express and share the rich history and culture of my country through dance. Participating in such celebrations helped me feel connected to my roots and community.

Children's Day was another special event where I used to perform folkloric dances when I was younger. Dancing on these occasions allowed me to celebrate Mexican culture alongside my friends and family. These experiences remain some of my fondest memories from my childhood in Mexico.

Father's Day was meaningful to me, especially because my dad was the one who introduced me to Mexican folkloric dance. I even performed folkloric dances during Father's Day celebrations, which made the day even more special. My dad's support has been a big part of my connection to Mexican culture.

Cinco de Mayo was one of the key events where I performed Mexican folkloric dances. Celebrating this day through dance helped me honor Mexican history and traditions. It was always a joyful occasion to share with my community and friends.

Mother's Day was another important celebration where I performed folkloric dances in Mexico. These events allowed me to express gratitude and love through cultural traditions. Dancing on Mother's Day connected me with my family and the broader Mexican community.

entity: Palace of Fine Arts, Mexico City
summary: The Palace of Fine Arts in Mexico City holds a special place in my heart because my dad took me there for my eleventh birthday to see the Ballet Folklórico de México. That visit sparked my love for Mexican folkloric dance, which has been a big part of my life since then. It was an unforgettable experience that deepened my connection to my culture.

The Ballet Folklórico de México inspired me deeply after I saw their performance at the Palace of Fine Arts when I was eleven. Their dances showed me the beauty and history of Mexican folklore, which motivated me to start dancing myself. This group has been a key influence in my cultural and artistic journey.

Minneapolis is where I currently live with my family after immigrating from Mexico. Here, I connected with a local ballet folklore group called Escencia Mexico, to which I lent my pink folkloric dress. I hope to join this group this coming summer and continue practicing the dance I love in this city.

Escencia Mexico is a ballet folklore group based in Minneapolis, and one of my friends who is part of it borrowed my pink folkloric dress. I hope to join Escencia Mexico this coming summer to continue dancing and celebrating Mexican culture here in the United States. This group represents a way for me to stay connected to my heritage while living far from Mexico.
